tasks.title: Retrieve relevant documents and data on Booking.com refund complaints
Search latest information and policies on Booking.com refunds and complaint handling
Analyze case details and identify reasons for refund not being booked
Formulate resolution steps and communication plan for customer refund escalation
Compile final report and recommended actions for Booking.com refund complaint triage

rag_output.summary:
- Refunds should not be promised for non-refundable bookings unless there is duplicate charging, a property error, or inconsistent policy display at booking [1].  
- Determine who collected the payment; if the property charged directly, gather payment receipts, message trails, and cancellation timelines before proceeding [2].  
- Escalate medium-risk refund complaints involving no-shows, unclear property policies, or incomplete payment evidence per the escalation matrix.  

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] payment_disputes.md, [3] escalation_matrix.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the booking is non-refundable, support should not promise a refund. The case can still be reviewed if there is duplicate charging, a property-side error, or evidence that the policy shown at booking was inconsistent.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

evidence.title: escalation_matrix.md
evidence.excerpt: Medium risk: refund complaints involving no-show disputes, unclear property policy, or incomplete payment evidence.

web_output.summary:
Task: How to submit a complaint or report a technical issue on Booking.com

Public evidence:
- Booking.com partners can submit complaints or report technical issues through a dedicated support contact form available on their partner help page.
- The complaint submission process involves providing detailed information about the issue to facilitate resolution.
- Booking.com has a structured complaints handling procedure to address and resolve partner concerns efficiently.

Sources: [1] https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/support-contact/contact/submitting-complaint-or-reporting-technical-issue, [2] https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/complaints-handling

evidence.title: Submitting a complaint or reporting a technical issue
evidence.excerpt: You can report a technical issue with our platform via the Extranet or Pulse app, and a member of our team will be in touch as soon as possible.

evidence.title: Complaints handling | Booking.com for Partners
evidence.excerpt: Mediation is an extension of our internal complaint handling process and is available for all partners and properties based in the EEA.

evidence.title: Know your rights: Flight delayed compensation
evidence.excerpt: Know your rights: Flight delayed compensation · EU regulations entitle passengers to compensation of up to €600 for delays over three hours ...

action_plan:
- Verify the booking’s refund eligibility by confirming payment collection details, checking for duplicate charges, property errors, or policy inconsistencies; collect all relevant payment receipts, message trails, and cancellation timelines if the property charged directly.  
- If the refund request involves medium-risk factors such as no-shows, unclear policies, or incomplete payment evidence, escalate the case according to the internal escalation matrix for further review.  
- Advise the customer or property partner to submit a detailed complaint or technical issue report via Booking.com’s partner support contact form to ensure proper documentation and facilitate resolution through the official complaints handling process.
next_steps:
1. Confirm payment collection details by verifying whether Booking.com or the property charged the customer; if the property charged directly, request all payment receipts, message trails, and cancellation timelines from the property partner.  
2. Check booking eligibility for a refund by reviewing the booking’s refund policy, looking for duplicate charges, property errors, or policy inconsistencies.  
3. If the case involves medium-risk factors such as no-shows, unclear policies, or incomplete payment evidence, escalate the issue following the internal escalation matrix for further assessment.  
4. Advise the customer or property partner to submit a detailed complaint or technical issue report via Booking.com’s partner support contact form to ensure proper documentation and facilitate resolution through the official complaints process.
final_answer:
Issue:  
The customer is escalating a refund request that has not been processed. The booking in question is potentially non-refundable, and the refund has not been automatically booked.

Internal policy:  
Refunds for non-refundable bookings should not be promised unless there is evidence of duplicate charging, a property error, or inconsistent policy display at the time of booking. It is essential to determine who collected the payment—if the property charged directly, all payment receipts, message trails, and cancellation timelines must be gathered before proceeding. Medium-risk refund complaints involving no-shows, unclear property policies, or incomplete payment evidence must be escalated according to the internal escalation matrix.

Public policy:  
Booking.com partners can submit complaints or report technical issues through a dedicated support contact form on the partner help page. This process requires detailed information to facilitate resolution and follows a structured complaints handling procedure to address partner concerns efficiently.

Recommended action:  
1. Verify who collected the payment for the booking. If the property charged directly, request all relevant payment documentation and communication records.  
2. Review the booking’s refund eligibility by checking for duplicate charges, property errors, or inconsistencies in the displayed policy.  
3. If the refund request involves medium-risk factors such as no-shows, unclear policies, or incomplete evidence, escalate the case per the internal escalation matrix.  
4. Advise the customer or property partner to submit a detailed complaint or technical issue report via Booking.com’s partner support contact form to ensure proper documentation and facilitate resolution.

Next steps:  
- Confirm payment collection details and gather all necessary documentation from the property if applicable.  
- Assess the booking’s refund eligibility based on internal policy criteria.  
- Escalate the case if medium-risk factors are identified.  
- Guide the customer or property partner to use the official Booking.com partner support contact form for complaint submission to ensure the issue is formally recorded and addressed.

Cited Sources:
- https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/support-contact/contact/submitting-complaint-or-reporting-technical-issue
